claims.title: _mark_auto_resolved() clears suppression fields unconditionally for all issues including suppressed ones
claims.detail: merge_issues.py:51-55 unconditionally sets issue['suppressed']=False, issue['suppressed_at']=None, issue['suppression_pattern']=None. auto_resolve_disappeared() has no guard to skip suppressed issues before calling _mark_auto_resolved(). A suppressed issue (suppressed=True, status='open') that falls outside the current scan_path will have _mark_auto_resolved() called at line 111-113, clearing all three suppression fields. The same applies to suppressed issues that genuinely disappear from scan at lines 122-131. The missing guard: there is no `if previous.get('suppressed'): continue` anywhere in auto_resolve_disappeared().

claims.title: Suppression loss is permanent
claims.detail: The suppression is not permanently lost. On the next scan where the issue reappears within scope, upsert_issues() at lines 160-162 re-evaluates matched_ignore_pattern(). If the ignore pattern still exists in .sloppyignore, suppression is re-applied (lines 191-195). However, the re-suppression path hits `continue` before the reopening check at line 201, leaving the issue in an inconsistent state: {status: 'auto_resolved', suppressed: True}. If the pattern no longer matches, the issue is correctly reopened. 'Permanent' is inaccurate — loss is temporary between the narrow-scope scan and the next broad scan.

claims.title: Inconsistent state after suppression recovery
claims.detail: When a previously-suppressed+auto-resolved issue reappears and the ignore pattern still matches, upsert_issues() sets suppressed=True but continues before reaching the status reopen check. The issue ends up with {status: 'auto_resolved', suppressed: True} — a mixed state not anticipated by the schema. match_issues() filters by `not issue.get('suppressed')` so the issue is hidden, but its status is semantically wrong. This is a consequence of the original flaw not raised by the submission.

fix_description: Add `if previous.get('suppressed'): continue` in auto_resolve_disappeared() immediately after the suspect-detector guard (after line 102). Suppressed issues should not be auto-resolved by a scan — they were explicitly suppressed by the user via ignore patterns. This prevents _mark_auto_resolved() from being called on suppressed issues in both the out-of-scope path and the normal disappear path.
notes: The core claim is valid: _mark_auto_resolved() has no suppression guard and clears suppression metadata on all issues it touches, including explicitly suppressed ones. The missing guard is a one-line fix. The 'permanent' characterization is inaccurate (suppression is recovered on the next in-scope scan), and the submission did not identify the secondary inconsistent-state consequence. No scoring engine impact — suppression state is orthogonal to score computation.
